forecasts.reasoning: Based on comprehensive searching through the knowledge cutoff date of April 25, 2025, I found no announcements or credible reports of any company being dropped from the Dow Jones Industrial Average. The most recent DJIA change announcement was November 1, 2024 (Nvidia replacing Intel and Sherwin-Williams replacing Dow Inc., effective November 8, 2024). I searched the official S&P Dow Jones Indices press releases, financial news sources, and market commentary with date filtering to pre-April 25, 2025, and found no evidence of any planned component changes. While DJIA changes can occur without long lead times, the short 2-month window from the knowledge cutoff to July 1, 2025 reduces probability. Historical DJIA change frequency is occasional but not scheduled. Without any rumors or signals as of the cutoff date, the probability of a new announcement drops in this window is roughly 18% (reflecting a small chance of surprise announcements in the remaining 2 months period).

While the IRS was reportedly "making plans" to revoke Harvard's 501(c)(3) status as of mid-April 2025, and sources suggested a decision "could come in days" on April 18, several factors make actual loss of status before July 1 unlikely: (1) Revocation requires a complex multi-stage administrative process with appeal rights that typically takes months to years; (2) The IRS has strong statutory independence protections against political interference; (3) Harvard would immediately challenge any revocation in federal court, likely obtaining a stay; (4) Historical precedent (Bob Jones University) shows the process took 7 years from initial revocation to final Supreme Court resolution; (5) No confirmed revocation had occurred by April 25, leaving only 66 days to the deadline. The threat appears to be serious political pressure rather than a legally credible process that could complete by July 1.

From April 25, 2025 to July 1, 2025 is only 67 days. Africa has had 8 successful coups since 2020 (averaging ~2/year), but spread across 54 countries with no baseline rate for such a short window. Latin America has had no successful coups in recent years - only failed attempts (Peru 2022, Brazil 2023, Bolivia 2024), and experts note military coups have become dramatically less frequent there. Major coup risk countries like Burkina Faso, Mali, Niger are already under juntas (protecting against new coups). South Sudan is in crisis but trending toward civil war, not coup. Only Burkina Faso shows 'warning signs' per April 2025 analysis. With this short timeframe and no active coup events underway as of April 25, the probability is moderate but not high. Approximately 37% probability reflects: (1) continued coup-prone environment in West Africa, (2) Chad had a failed attempt in Jan 2025 suggesting ongoing plotting, (3) Guinea's 2-year transition timeline from 2022 means elections should occur soon, creating tension, but (4) Latin America essentially zero probability and (5) most high-risk African countries already under military control.
